kill_fact: The bear case's load-bearing pillar -- 'the stock at ~$461 trades ABOVE the analyst median price target (~$304) and ABOVE even the high target (~$332), so the street is MORE bearish than the price and the consensus delta runs against the buyer' -- is FALSE on current data. As of June 2026 the consensus average price target is ~$442-455 (median ~$459, stockanalysis.com, 7 analysts), with the high at ~$503-564. Multiple analysts RAISED targets sharply in May 2026: Stifel $351->$503, Clear Street $400->$500, Baird $373->$450. The '$304 median / $332 high' the moderator leaned on is a stale, low-coverage snapshot (superseded by the post-Q1-26 re-rate). Sell-side targets now bracket/exceed the $461 price rather than sitting ~33% below it, so the central de-rate/over-priced-vs-street argument does not hold against a primary read of current consensus. Underlying financials all verify (Q1-26 revenue $1.0B +20%, NI $46.8M, EPS $2.99, GM 13.4%, backlog $2.84B = $981M T&D + $1.86B C&I, cash $163M, debt $9.4M, 0.04x leverage, 15.57M shares, price $461.10 on 2026-06-18) -- the kill is to the BEAR'S valuation framing, not to the numbers.
corrections: Replace the stale '$304 median / $332 high' analyst-target anchor with current consensus (avg ~$442-455, median ~$459, high ~$503-564; Stifel $503, Clear Street $500, Baird $450 raised May-2026). The 'priced above the street' claim must be dropped. RESIDUAL bear points that DO verify and should be retained: (1) Q1 13.4% gross margin is partly non-durable -- the 10-Q attributes it to 'favorable change orders and job closeouts' + net estimate changes (+0.8% / $8.5M op income), so the durable run-rate is the lower guide bands; (2) valuation is genuinely rich/top-of-cohort (~27x EV/EBITDA vs PRIM ~16x) and the re-rate catalyst has largely fired (+176%/52wk); (3) the thesis is single-factor (AI-capex/electrification) with no idiosyncratic resolution driver -- full correlation to the rest of scale-out Basket B. These cap conviction but are not disqualifiers.
evidence: stockanalysis.com/stocks/myrg/forecast/ (fetched 2026-06-22): MYRG analyst consensus avg target ~$455, median ~$459.5, high ~$564, low ~$295; 7 analysts, Buy -- i.e. targets bracket/exceed the $461 price, NOT ~$304.
CNN Markets / Benzinga analyst-rating feed (search 2026-06-22): May-2026 target raises -- Stifel $351->$503, Clear Street $400->$500, Baird $373->$450 -- post-Q1-26 upward re-rate of price targets.

MYR Group Q1-2026 8-K / 10-Q via SEC EDGAR (CIK 0000700923, quarter ended 2026-03-31): revenue $1,000.4M (+20.0% YoY), net income $46.8M, diluted EPS $2.99, gross margin 13.4% (from 11.6%), EBITDA $81.5M, record backlog $2.84B ($981M T&D + $1.86B C&I), cash $163M, total debt $9.4M, 0.04x leverage; 10-Q attributes margin to 'favorable change orders and job closeouts' (close-out flatter confirmed).

stockanalysis.com/stocks/myrg/ (fetched 2026-06-22): price $461.10 as of 2026-06-18, market cap $7.18B, 15.57M shares, P/E 50.76, 52wk $165.00-$484.71 -- price/share-count anchors in the debate file verified.
